item.title: Gautam Adani will seek to dismiss US SEC fraud case
item.description: Gautam Adani, one of вҖӢIndia's richest people, вҖҢwill ask a judge to dismiss the U.S. вҖӢSecurities and Exchange вҖӢCommission's civil fraud case вҒ against him, his вҖӢlawyers said on Tuesday.

item.title: From falling U.S. wealth to Indian factory closures, oil shock raises global recession risk
item.description: Kevin Kelly is in a tough spot. What he says are unprecedented price increases in the weeks since the United States waged war on Iran mean the Californian, who makes plastic bags for groceries, may have to break contracts he cannot afford to honor with his customers.

item.title: USTR Greer says US-Mexico-Canada pact talks may run past July 1 deadline
item.description: The Trump administration will try to resolve as many problems with the вҖӢU.S.-Mexico-Canada trade agreement as it can before July 1, вҖҢbut negotiations to rebalance the trade pact are likely to continue past that deadline, U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said on Tuesday.

item.title: Deal, delay or strike? Investors on edge as Trump's Iran deadline nears
item.description: Global markets have entered a period of heightened uncertainty ahead of a deadline set by U.S. President Donald Trump for Iran, with investors weighing various outcomes ranging from a ceasefire to renewed military escalation and their implications for oil, currencies and risky assets.

item.title: Heads of IEA, IMF, World Bank to meet next Monday to discuss energy crisis
item.description: The leaders of the International Energy Agency, the International Monetary Fund and the World вҖӢBank will discuss the energy crisis triggered by вҖҢthe Iran war next Monday, IEA executive director Fatih Birol said on Tuesday.

item.title: Vape makers turn to 'Made in America' credentials amid Trump's tariffs, crackdown
item.description: The U.S. vaping market, long dominated by Chinese imports, is seeing an uptick of "Made in America" products, in what some analysts and industry executives вҖӢsay is a reaction to the Trump administration's crackdown on unlicensed brands.

item.title: Southwest Airlines to limit passengers to one portable charger on flights
item.description: Southwest Airlines said Tuesday it will вҖӢlimit passengers to one вҖҢlithium portable charger on flights and ban them from вҖӢbeing stowed in вҖӢoverhead bins to reduce risks вҒ of inflight battery вҖӢfires.

item.title: US lawmakers press Bessent on LNG ships that are taking tax credit
item.description: A group of Democratic U.S. senators on Tuesday pressed Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on whether enormous tankers вҖӢthat ship liquefied natural gas qualify for a lucrative tax вҖҢcredit meant for smaller boats that burn the fuel.

item.title: NY Fed March survey finds jump in near-term inflation expectations
item.description: Americans, rattled by surging energy prices вҖӢtied to war in the Middle East, are expecting higher near-term inflation and fresh challenges to their вҖҢpersonal financial situations, the Federal Reserve Bank of New York reported on Tuesday.

item.title: Physical oil prices hit record highs near $150 a barrel as Hormuz crisis worsens
item.description: European and Asian refiners are paying record high prices вҖҢof near $150 a barrel for some crude oil grades, far exceeding prices for paper futures, highlighting the worsening supply crisis from the U.S.-Israel war with Iran.

item.title: Hot money increasingly dominates emerging markets financing, raising risks, IMF says
item.description: Emerging market вҖҢnations now get the bulk of their foreign financing from the likes of hedge funds, pension funds and insurers, leaving them vulnerable to rapid outflows during crises, the International Monetary Fund said in a report.

item.title: Intel to join Musk's Terafab mega AI chip project
item.description: Intel вҖӢsaid вҖҢon вҖӢTuesday вҖӢit вҒ would вҖӢjoin вҖӢElon Musk's вҖӢTerafab вҖӢAI chip вҖҢcomplex вҒ project вҖӢalong вҖӢwith вҒ SpaceX, вҖӢTesla вҖӢand вҒ xAI.

item.title: Delta Air hikes checked baggage fees as jet fuel prices soar
item.description: Delta Air Lines said on Tuesday it will raise fees for checked bags on domestic and select short-haul international вҖӢroutes, as airlines seek to offset soaring jet fuel вҖҢcosts linked to escalating tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: Moody's cuts outlook on US BDCs to 'negative' on redemption pressure, rising leverage
item.description: Moody's Ratings on Tuesday revised its outlook on U.S. business development companies to negative вҖӢfrom stable, citing rising redemption pressures, higher leverage вҖҢand weakening access to funding markets.

item.title: Uber bets on Amazon's custom chips to boost AI efforts
item.description: Uber is using Amazon's custom chips to speed up вҖӢcomputing and train artificial intelligence вҖҢmodels, the cloud giant said on Tuesday, as the ride-hailing вҖӢfirm seeks advanced hardware вҖӢto handle growing digital workloads.

item.title: Fed's Williams says Middle East war will drive up inflation - Bloomberg
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of вҖӢNew York President John вҖҢWilliams said Tuesday the Middle East war energy shock will вҖӢdrive up overall вҖӢinflation over the course of вҒ this year, while reiterating вҖӢmonetary policy is in вҖӢthe right place to deal with what happens in the economy.

item.title: US core capital goods orders increase in February
item.description: New orders for key U.S.-manufactured capital goods вҖӢincreased more than expected вҖҢin February, but data for the prior month was вҖӢrevised sharply lower, suggesting вҖӢsome moderation in business spending вҒ on equipment in the вҖӢfirst quarter.

item.title: Russia says Middle East war disruption opens up new trade opportunities
item.description: Global supply disruptions caused by the вҖӢwar in the Middle East have opened up вҖҢnew trade opportunities for Russia, but price stability in the domestic market remains a priority, Russian Prime Minister Mikhail Mishustin вҖӢsaid on Tuesday.

item.title: Gilead to buy Germany's Tubulis for up to $5 billion to boost cancer pipeline
item.description: Gilead said on Tuesday it would acquire private Germany-based Tubulis GmbH for up вҖӢto $5 billion, as the U.S. drugmaker looks to boost its вҖҢpipeline with a lucrative class of experimental cancer drugs referred to as "guided missiles".

item.title: Puig, Estee Lauder founding families to meet for combination talks, source says
item.description: The founding вҖҢfamilies of the Spanish firm Puig , which owns such brands as Rabanne and Nina Ricci, and U.S. cosmetics giant Estee Lauder will meet this week in New York to negotiate the вҖӢterms of a potential business combination, a person with knowledge of the вҖӢtalks said on Tuesday.

item.title: Novo Nordisk launches higher-dose Wegovy in the United States
item.description: Novo Nordisk said on Tuesday that it has made a higher-dose вҖӢversion of its blockbuster weight-loss drug вҖҢWegovy available across the United States.

item.title: US stock futures inch lower as investors assess Mideast developments
item.description: U.S. stock index futures ticked lower on вҖӢTuesday as investors assessed comments that indicated the Middle East conflict could escalate, ahead of President Donald Trump's deadline вҖҢfor Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Fox to integrate Kalshi prediction market data across news platforms
item.description: Fox Corp said on Tuesday it will integrate Kalshi's вҖӢprediction market data across its вҖҢnews and streaming platforms, reflecting a broader trend among media organizations to вҖӢuse market-based probabilities to complement вҖӢtraditional reporting.

item.title: AI networking firm Aria Networks raises $125 million in funding
item.description: Aria Networks said on Tuesday it has raised $125 million in its first series funding round, as the startup вҖӢseeks to develop its AI networking infrastructure to meet the вҖҢsoaring demand for capacity amid the rapid adoption of artificial intelligence.

item.title: ASML shares fall on US Congress plan to further restrict China exports
item.description: Shares of computer chip equipment maker ASML dipped on Tuesday, the first trading day after U.S. lawmakers last week proposed a law that could further restrict the company's sales to вҖӢChina.

item.title: Morning Bid: Final countdown?
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today

item.title: Some firms in Saudi extend work from home ahead of Iran ultimatum
item.description: Some Western and Saudi firms in Riyadh have extended work-from-home advisories this week, five people familiar with the matter вҖӢsaid, amid concern over a looming U.S.-imposed deadline for Iran вҖҢto open the Strait of Hormuz or face "hell".

item.title: UBS lowers 2026 S&P 500 target on Middle East conflict risks
item.description: UBS Global Wealth Management trimmed its S&P 500 index target for 2026, pointing to sustained higher oil вҖӢprices amid the ongoing Middle East conflict that could pressure U.S. вҖҢeconomic growth and inflation.

item.title: Bourbon demand is down and tariffs aren't helping. But distillers keep building.
item.description: Like many whiskey distillers, Heaven Hill Brands is rolling back bourbon production this year, as demand lags. Yet, as the American spirit faced a tumultuous market in 2025, the Kentucky company built a new, $200 million distillery in the heart of bourbon country, adding 155,000 barrels of capacity.

item.title: Blockbuster SpaceX listing could suck the oxygen out of fragile IPO market
item.description: As Elon Musk's SpaceX closes in on a $75 billion IPO that could rewrite record books, concerns are mounting that others looking to list in 2026 may find it harder to get deals done under the shadow of the space venture's headline-grabbing debut.
